title: HDEL
description: Deletes one or more fields and their values from a hash. Deletes the hash if no fields remain.
complexity: O(N) where N is the number of fields to be removed.

returns.description: The number of fields that were removed from the hash, not including specified but non-existing fields. If key does not exist, it is treated as an empty hash and this command returns 0.

jedis.signature: long hdel(final byte[] key, final byte[]... fields)

returns.description: The number of fields that were removed from the hash, not including specified but non-existing fields. If key does not exist, it is treated as an empty hash and this command returns 0.

jedis.signature: long hdel(final String key, final String... fields)

returns.description: Long integer-reply the number of fields that were removed from the hash, not including specified but non existing fields.
Long integer-reply the number of fields that were removed from the hash, not including specified but non existing fields.
Long integer-reply the number of fields that were removed from the hash, not including specified but non existing fields.

returns.description: The number of fields that were removed.

returns.description: the number of fields that were removed from the hash

content:
Removes the specified fields from the hash stored at `key`. Specified fields that do not exist within this hash are ignored. Deletes the hash if no fields remain. If `key` does not exist, it is treated as an empty hash and this command returns `0`. ## Examples {{< clients-example set="cmds_hash" step="hdel" description="Foundational: Delete one or more fields from a hash using HDEL (returns count of deleted fields, ignores non-existent fields)" difficulty="beginner" >}} HSET myhash field1 "foo" (integer) 1 HDEL myhash field1 (integer) 1 HDEL myhash field2 (integer) 0 {{< /clients-example >}} Give these commands a try in the interactive console: {{% redis-cli %}} HSET myhash field1 "foo" HDEL myhash field1 HDEL myhash field2 {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="hdel-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of fields that were removed from the hash, excluding any specified but non-existing fields. -tab-sep- [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): The number of fields that were removed from the hash, excluding any specified but non-existing fields. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
HSET myhash field1 "foo"
(integer) 1
HDEL myhash field1
(integer) 1
HDEL myhash field2
(integer) 0
intro:
Removes the specified fields from the hash stored at `key`.
Specified fields that do not exist within this hash are ignored.
Deletes the hash if no fields remain.
If `key` does not exist, it is treated as an empty hash and this command returns
`0`.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Removes the specified fields from the hash stored at `key`.
Specified fields that do not exist within this hash are ignored.
Deletes the hash if no fields remain.
If `key` does not exist, it is treated as an empty hash and this command returns
`0`.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
{{< clients-example set="cmds_hash" step="hdel" description="Foundational: Delete one or more fields from a hash using HDEL (returns count of deleted fields, ignores non-existent fields)" difficulty="beginner" >}}
HSET myhash field1 "foo"
(integer) 1
HDEL myhash field1
(integer) 1
HDEL myhash field2
(integer) 0
{{< /clients-example >}}

Give these commands a try in the interactive console:

{{% redis-cli %}}
HSET myhash field1 "foo"
HDEL myhash field1
HDEL myhash field2
{{% /redis-cli %}}

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |
